suggested_titles.title: Verdant Echoes
suggested_titles.subtitle: An Abstract Landscape
suggested_titles.description: A compelling abstract piece featuring deep greens punctuated by vibrant red and blue geometric forms, suggesting a rich, contemplative landscape with a sense of depth and reflection.

suggested_titles.title: Horizon Lines
suggested_titles.subtitle: Floating Fields Series
suggested_titles.description: Dynamic white lines traverse an expansive green field, framing abstract geometric elements that seem to float or reflect, inviting a serene yet intriguing visual experience.

suggested_titles.title: Deep Green Respite
suggested_titles.subtitle: Abstract Contemplation
suggested_titles.description: A minimalist abstract work dominated by a lush green hue, accented by striking red and blue shapes and fluid white strokes, evoking quiet contemplation and visual stillness.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork with dramatic contemporary flair, emphasizing its physical presence and monumental scale within a clean, minimalist architectural space, suitable for a sophisticated collector or commercial gallery setting.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Minimalist contemporary art gallery or refined home-gallery space.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Clean, calm, and architecturally pristine, with a contemplative and slightly imposing mood due to the unique perspective. Neutral to cool, with the artwork as the vibrant focal point.
materials: Matte off-white mineral wall with subtle texture
Polished concrete floor
Simple ceiling plane
Discreet gallery track lighting
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft gallery daylight filtering from unseen high windows, complemented by focused, cool track lighting subtly illuminating the artwork's surface from above, creating gentle highlights and shadows.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned extremely low, approximately 5-15 cm from the ground, with the lens nearly touching the floor. This is a mouse-level/nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint, not a gentle low 3/4. The floor plane must be large and close in the foreground while wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals rise strongly. Use an assertive oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one forceful vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. The wall, floor, ceiling, and artwork should feel pulled into the same steep perspective. Use the nadir effect strongly through the surrounding room geometry: a large foreground floor wedge, floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint must be obvious at thumbnail size. Do not produce a polite standing-height gallery view.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the dramatic foreground floor to lead into the rising wall and artwork, showcasing depth while keeping the artwork dominant.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: This extremely low, upward-looking camera angle dramatically enhances the artwork's physical presence, making it appear more monumental and imposing. It creates a dynamic visual journey from the floor up to the canvas, highlighting its installation in a real architectural space and emphasizing its canvas thickness.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's minimalist composition and deep, contemplative green are exceptionally highlighted by the stark, clean lines and neutral tones of a contemporary gallery. The unique 'Nadir Controlado' camera perspective lends an unexpected monumentality to the piece, inviting viewers to experience it from a fresh, powerful viewpoint, aligning with cutting-edge art display trends.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This presentation immediately positions the artwork as a premium piece suitable for high-end contemporary spaces. The dramatic camera angle and minimalist setting create a sophisticated, memorable visual that appeals to discerning collectors and interior designers seeking impactful statements. It underscores the artwork's value and visual strength.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: An abstract painting, featuring a deep green field with red and blue geometric shapes and subtle white wavy lines, is centrally displayed on a matte off-white gallery wall. The artwork is presented from an extremely low 'nadir controlado' camera perspective, approximately 5-15 cm from the polished concrete floor, with the lens almost touching the ground. The camera looks strongly upward and obliquely across the room, creating a dramatic, forceful vanishing perspective. A large wedge of the polished concrete floor fills the foreground, with the clean, neutral off-white wall and the artwork rising prominently in the midground. Discreet gallery track lighting is visible on the simple ceiling plane, providing soft, cool daylight illumination that highlights the artwork's texture and forms. The overall atmosphere is clean, calm, and architecturally refined, emphasizing the artwork's physical presence and scale from this unique viewpoint. No visible people or animals. Preserve artwork exactly. Ensure canvas thickness is visible from the low angle.

contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned extremely low, approximately 5-15 cm from the ground, with the lens nearly touching the floor. This is a mouse-level/nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint, not a gentle low 3/4. The floor plane must be large and close in the foreground while wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals rise strongly. Use an assertive oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one forceful vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. The wall, floor, ceiling, and artwork should feel pulled into the same steep perspective. Use the nadir effect strongly through the surrounding room geometry: a large foreground floor wedge, floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint must be obvious at thumbnail size. Do not produce a polite standing-height gallery view.

contextual_proposals.context_name: Verdant Contemplation in a Collector's Study
contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork as the focal point within a discerning private collection, emphasizing its contemplative depth and material presence from a unique, grounded perspective.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Quiet collector's study or intimate living room corner.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Serene, intimate, and introspective. Soft, warm domestic light filters into a cultivated space, creating a calm backdrop for quiet appreciation. A sense of grounded stability from the low perspective.
materials: Warm limewash plaster wall
Dark natural oak floorboards
Subtle textured wool rug in muted earth tones
Minimalist black metal or dark wood low console (partially cropped)
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, diffused morning light streaming in from a tall, narrow window, casting gentle, elongated shadows from the low perspective. The artwork is evenly illuminated, with subtle depth from the side light.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is aggressively low, 5-20 cm from the floor, clearly far below knee height. The viewpoint must feel like the lens is near the floor and close to the wall, not standing height, not chest height, and not a normal eye-level gallery view. Use a very strong 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork side depth, floor plane, and room volume rush toward one shared vanishing direction. The side of the canvas can feel dramatically deep through perspective, while the artwork remains a rigid object. The artwork is seen with aggressive side depth and believable thickness from a near-floor 7/8 viewpoint. Include a large visible floor wedge or floor plane in the lower foreground, with wall/floor junctions and architectural verticals proving the camera is below knee height. Use strong asymmetry, a close foreground edge, and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera. This must feel almost perspective-deformed and must not collapse into a standard standing-height 3/4 gallery photograph.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, showcasing the artwork as dominant while revealing the architectural depth from the unique low perspective.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The extremely low 7/8 perspective dramatically exaggerates the physical thickness of the canvas, making it appear robust and dimensional. The expansive floor plane and upward converging lines of the room emphasize the artwork's monumental presence, grounding it firmly within the collector's space and preventing a flat, poster-like appearance. The asymmetry adds artistic tension.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's deep green, geometric forms, and contemplative lines find a resonant home in a quiet collector's study. The muted, warm limewash plaster walls provide a sophisticated, non-distracting backdrop, allowing the artwork's subtle complexity and vibrant contrasts to fully emerge. The intimate scale of the room reinforces the personal connection to the art, positioning it as a valued cultural artifact rather than mere decoration.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context elevates the artwork's perceived value by associating it with a refined, discerning private collection. The unique 'Contrapicado 7/8' camera view highlights its physical presence and substantiality, conveying a sense of permanence and quality. It targets buyers who appreciate art as an intellectual and aesthetic cornerstone of their living space, appealing to a premium market segment.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A large-scale contemporary abstract painting with a dominant deep emerald green background, vibrant red, pink, and turquoise geometric squares, and flowing white lines, is prominently wall-hung in a cultivated private collector's study. The wall is finished in warm limewash plaster with subtle texture. The floor is made of dark natural oak floorboards, partially covered by a muted earth-toned wool rug in the foreground. A minimalist, low black metal console is partially visible and cropped on the left, blending into the foreground. The entire scene is captured with a 'Contrapicado 7/8' camera perspective: aggressively low, 5-20 cm from the floor, with a very strong 7/8 oblique rotation, exaggerating depth and showcasing the artwork's physical thickness. A large visible floor wedge and wall/floor junctions are prominent, with architectural verticals rising sharply. Soft, diffused morning light streams in from a tall, narrow window, creating gentle shadows and illuminating the artwork evenly. The artwork is the cultural center, scale is private collector, intimate private-room scale, calm layout, decor subordinate to artwork. No visible people or animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: No people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default unless seasonally intended, no eye-level view, no standing-height view, no normal 3/4 gallery photo, no gentle low angle, no soft controlled viewpoint, no flat wall view, no melted artwork, no torn canvas, no artwork substitution.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is aggressively low, 5-20 cm from the floor, clearly far below knee height. The viewpoint must feel like the lens is near the floor and close to the wall, not standing height, not chest height, and not a normal eye-level gallery view. Use a very strong 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork side depth, floor plane, and room volume rush toward one shared vanishing direction. The side of the canvas can feel dramatically deep through perspective, while the artwork remains a rigid object. The artwork is seen with aggressive side depth and believable thickness from a near-floor 7/8 viewpoint. Include a large visible floor wedge or floor plane in the lower foreground, with wall/floor junctions and architectural verticals proving the camera is below knee height. Use strong asymmetry, a close foreground edge, and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera. This must feel almost perspective-deformed and must not collapse into a standard standing-height 3/4 gallery photograph.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork as a sophisticated focal point in a quiet luxury living space, enhancing its contemplative nature with warm, natural light and refined architectural depth.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Aspirational living room within a premium domestic residence or a large private study.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Serene, refined, quiet luxury. A warm, contemplative atmosphere infused with the soft, golden light of late afternoon, evoking polished calm and understated elegance.
materials: Limewash wall in a warm, neutral tone (e.g., pale taupe or ivory)
Wide-plank pale oak flooring
Subtle plaster ceiling with discreet coving
A low, linear console in natural light wood or a pale stone finish (e.g., travertine) positioned beneath the artwork
A refined, low-pile wool rug in a complementary neutral shade
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, golden afternoon light streaming in from a large window on the left, casting gentle, elongated shadows and highlighting the texture of the limewash wall and the artwork's surface. Low ambient light in the deeper parts of the room.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Reflejo Dorado de Tarde / Palazzo: Camera height is elegant human standing height, approximately 150-170 cm, as if observed calmly from inside a palazzo room. Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to be central while showcasing the elegant proportions and materials of the room.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The soft three-quarter rotation reveals the physical thickness of the canvas and creates architectural depth, leading the eye past the artwork into the subtle sophistication of the room. The elegant human-height perspective provides a natural, inviting view.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's deep green and vibrant red/blue elements gain a jewel-like quality under the golden afternoon light, resonating with the warm premium neutrals of the quiet luxury setting. The contemplative, expansive nature of the painting is mirrored by the serene, uncluttered domestic space, creating a harmonious and aspirational aesthetic.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context positions the artwork as a high-value, statement piece within a desirable, aspirational domestic setting, appealing to discerning collectors who appreciate understated elegance and refined living. The warm, natural light enhances the artwork's visual appeal and suggests an integrated, sophisticated lifestyle.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: An XL horizontal artwork, deep emerald green with red and blue abstract shapes and white lines, is wall-mounted in a refined quiet luxury living room. The room features warm neutral limewash walls, wide-plank pale oak flooring, and a low travertine console beneath the artwork. Soft, golden afternoon light streams from a large window to the left, casting a gentle glow across the wall and artwork. The camera is at elegant human standing height (150-170 cm), looking with a soft three-quarter rotation, revealing architectural depth and warm, stable composition. The artwork is the focal point, balanced within the room. No people, no animals, wall-hanging artwork.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no influencer lifestyle, no real estate ad, no hotel catalog look, no furniture showroom
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Reflejo Dorado de Tarde / Palazzo: Camera height is elegant human standing height, approximately 150-170 cm, as if observed calmly from inside a palazzo room. Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.

contextual_proposals.context_role: Elevates the artwork's status by placing it in a grand historical setting, offering a unique, aspirational viewpoint that emphasizes scale and architectural dialogue.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Grand room within a European palazzo or stately historical residence
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Refined historical grandeur, quiet contemplation, warm and serene late afternoon, bridging classical architecture with modern abstraction.
materials: Aged plaster walls (muted cream or pale stone hue)
Warm, polished terrazzo floor with subtle geometric patterns
Restrained classical moulding on walls and ceiling
Dark polished wood (e.g., a low console or bench)
Heavy linen or raw silk drapes (seen subtly from above at windows)
Warm stone or marble accents (e.g., a simple pedestal)
contextual_proposals.lighting: Golden late day sunlight streaming softly through a tall arched window, creating gentle highlights on the floor and a warm, diffused glow across the artwork, with deep, soft shadows in corners.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Nadir Aéreo con Contexto de Ventanas: Camera is positioned very high, near ceiling height, upper balcony height, skylight height, or the top edge of a tall architectural void. It is the aerial counterpart of an extreme nadir: instead of looking upward from the floor, the camera looks downward from above into the room. Use an oblique high-corner or upper-balcony diagonal, not a centered frontal wall view. The camera may look diagonally down along the room so window context, floor geometry, and artwork wall are visible together. Build the image from above: visible floor plane, furniture or bench tops, rug or floor layout, wall height, window/skylight context, and the artwork installed on a vertical wall. The artwork remains identifiable, but the room must read as seen from above, not as a normal wall presentation.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to be central while clearly showing the grandeur and depth of the space from the elevated viewpoint.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The high, downward oblique angle dramatically captures the grand scale of the palazzo, emphasizing the room's depth and architectural details like the tall windows and intricate floor patterns from a unique, elevated perspective. The artwork, while seen from above on a vertical plane, maintains its presence and shows its physical thickness, grounding it within the stately historical context. This view transforms the space into a structured composition, making the artwork a focal point within a larger, aspirational design narrative.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's minimalist abstraction and deep green hue create a compelling visual counterpoint to the rich, aged textures and classical proportions of the palazzo. This dialogue accentuates the timeless quality of both the art and the architecture, allowing the artwork's quiet intensity to resonate within a space of refined historical gravity. The contrast between contemporary art and old-world elegance enriches the perception of both.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: Positions the artwork as a high-value piece suitable for discerning collectors with grand historical properties or contemporary spaces inspired by classicism. The dramatic, elevated view from above, emphasizing the room's expansive scale and luxurious materials, elevates the artwork's perceived status, making it appear both significant and perfectly integrated into an aspirational, refined environment. This framing suggests the artwork is substantial enough to hold its own in a culturally rich setting.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: Artwork: abstract green painting with red/blue squares and white lines, exact format, proportions, colors, surface texture, and thickness. No cropping or alteration. Placement: Artwork installed prominently on a vertical wall within a grand European palazzo room. Scene: A spacious, old European palazzo room with tall proportions. Walls are aged plaster in a muted historic cream, featuring subtle classical moulding. The floor is warm, polished terrazzo with intricate subtle patterns, extending from the foreground into the mid-ground, seen prominently from above. In the mid-ground, a low, dark polished wooden bench or simple console is positioned, observed from the high aerial perspective, contributing to the room's layout. In the background or along a visible side wall, the upper part of a very tall arched window or a series of arched openings allows golden late afternoon light to stream in, creating soft highlights on the terrazzo floor and a gentle, warm glow across the artwork. Lighting: Golden late day light, soft and warm, originating from the tall window context, illuminating the floor and grazing the artwork. Camera: Nadir Aéreo con Contexto de Ventanas view, positioned very high, looking diagonally downward from an elevated architectural vantage point (e.g., upper mezzanine or high archway), capturing the prominent terrazzo floor, the low dark wood bench, the vertical wall with the artwork, and the upper section of the tall arched window in the background, all rendered with a deep, converging perspective from above. The artwork's physical thickness is clearly visible, ensuring it reads as a real canvas. No visible people, no animals.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork in a refined studio environment, emphasizing its material authenticity and the contemplative space of its creation, while showcasing its substantial presence in an architectural passage.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Serene, contemplative, dedicated to artistic practice, infused with quiet daylight. Reflects creative refinement and a working, yet impeccably maintained, aesthetic.
materials: Plastered studio walls with subtle, matte white finish
Light-toned polished concrete floor
White-painted ceiling with visible, neat studio lighting tracks
Subtle, neatly stored wooden canvases or simple wood easel (blurred in background)
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, even north light filtering from large windows or skylights, illuminating the space with a cool, natural glow. The artwork is well-lit but without harsh shadows or direct sunbeams, enhancing its depth and color.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Pasillo, Obra Descentrada y Próxima: Camera height is natural standing or slightly lowered human height, close enough to feel the corridor edge. Use an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the artwork deliberately off-center and close rather than symmetrically framed. Place the artwork on a long wall plane rather than a short frontal display wall. The wall should extend laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, corridor, or elongated architectural plane, creating a clear vanishing direction. Use floor lines, ceiling lines, wall joints, lighting tracks, windows, columns, doorways, or distant wall planes to reinforce depth. The artwork may sit off-center and relatively close to the viewer, while the architecture recedes naturally to one side. Avoid product-style centered symmetry.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the architectural depth to be seen while keeping the artwork dominant.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The subtle three-quarter angle reveals the physical thickness of the canvas, making it feel like a substantial object. The off-center placement and receding architectural lines create dynamic depth, drawing the eye along the wall plane and emphasizing the artwork's commanding presence within a purposeful space. The focus remains sharply on the artwork, with a natural photographic blur on distant elements.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: This studio environment, with its quiet and spacious feel, aligns with the artwork's contemplative nature and expansive green field. It suggests the artwork is part of a serious creative practice, enhancing its perceived value and authenticity. The neutral, clean backdrop allows the artwork's colors and composition to stand out without competition.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: Presenting the artwork in a refined artist studio passage adds a layer of intellectual and creative gravitas, appealing to collectors who appreciate the origin and context of art. The dynamic camera angle and architectural depth offer a premium visual experience, differentiating it from flat, generic presentations and showcasing its potential in sophisticated residential or office settings that value art within a designed environment.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A large abstract painting, 'Untitled' (as provided image) with a deep green field and red/blue square elements, is hung slightly off-center on a long, subtly textured plaster wall in a clean, quiet artist's working studio passage. The wall extends laterally into the scene, creating a clear vanishing direction towards the right, revealing architectural depth. The floor is light-toned polished concrete. A neat, white-painted ceiling with subtle lighting tracks is visible. Soft, even north light illuminates the space from large, unseen windows or skylights, casting no harsh shadows. The camera is at natural standing height, angled in a three-quarter view, close enough to feel the architectural edge. The artwork is dominant, in sharp focus, showcasing its physical canvas and texture. Distant studio elements, such as a blurred stack of neatly stored canvases or a simple wooden stool, subtly recede out of focus in the background, hinting at creative activity without clutter. No people, no animals.

Deep muted burgundy-toned accent wall or wood paneling
